# Content Action Playbook — Refresh & Optimization Lane

This playbook translates the output of our machine learning models and heuristic scores into actionable, human-reviewed editorial guidelines. It is designed to assist marketing and content teams in prioritizing and executing content refreshes efficiently.

---

## 1. Intended Use & Non-Production Scope

*   **Decision-Support Tool**: This playbook is built exclusively to assist human editors and copywriters. It should not be integrated into any automated content publishing pipeline.
*   **Advisory Status**: Recommendations generated by the scoring engine represent statistical risks and correlation patterns; they do not dictate absolute performance truths. Every recommendation must undergo manual quality assurance before implementation.

---

## 2. Archetype-to-Action Mapping

We classify search performance pages into distinct archetypes to map them to targeted operational actions:

| Page Archetype | Performance Characteristics | Recommended Action |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Striking Distance Page** | Avg Rank between 10.0 and 20.0; high impressions but low CTR. | **Rewrite Metadata** (Optimize page title, meta description, and schema tags to capture CTR). |
| **High-Exposure Old Page** | Content age > 270 days; top 10 rankings; observed historical decay. | **Refresh Content** (Update statistics, add modern examples, and verify external links). |
| **Bloated Long-Form Article** | Word count > 2,000 words; high rank but downward CTR trend. | **Merge / Trim** (Prune low-value sections, resolve internal keyword cannibalization, or split into highly focused child pages). |
| **Low-Volume Inactive Page** | Impressions near zero; page age > 180 days. | **Prune / Monitor** (If irrelevant to core brand, delete and set up a 301 redirect; if relevant, monitor). |
| **High-Performance Asset** | Stable Top 3 rankings; high conversion metrics. | **Protect** (Monitor competitor actions, keep links updated, do not make structural content changes). |

---

## 3. Ranked Actions & Reason Codes

Every page in the prioritized queue is assigned a combination of an action and a primary reason code to guide the editorial team:

### Reason Codes:
*   **`DEC-01` (Traffic Decay)**: Measured organic clicks and impressions have declined by > 30% over the last 90 days.
*   **`POS-03` (Striking Distance Volatility)**: Page sits on the border of Page 1 and Page 2 (positions 9-15), where a slight optimization can yield massive traffic gains.
*   **`AGE-02` (Freshness Erosion)**: Content has not been updated in over 270 days and is losing ground to fresher competitor articles.
*   **`CTR-04` (Sub-optimal Engagement)**: High impressions but click-through rate is significantly below the expected position benchmark.

---

## 4. Human Review & Verification Rules

Before an editor implements any action suggested by the model, they must verify the following items:

1.  **Seasonality Check**:
    - *Rule*: Check the search term volume in Google Trends. If the decay is driven by natural season shifts (e.g. tax calculators in summer), **defer action** and label the page `monitor`.
2.  **Intent Alignment**:
    - *Rule*: Perform a live Google search for the primary target keyword. Verify if search intent has changed (e.g. from informational blog posts to transactional landing pages). If the intent has shifted, rewrite the page layout to match the new intent.
3.  **Render & Technical Health**:
    - *Rule*: Inspect the page URL in Google Search Console for Core Web Vitals or mobile rendering errors. Technical index errors should be resolved by developers before editors rewrite content.

---

## 5. What NOT to Automate (No-Go Cases)

The following actions carry high operational risk and **must never** be automated:

*   **Page Deletions & Redirects**: Deleting pages or applying automated 301 redirects can cause permanent loss of backlink equity and rank flags. These actions must always be executed manually by a senior webmaster.
*   **Brand Voice & Core Messaging**: Rewriting introductory hooks or main landing page copies using raw generative AI models without human editor approval is strictly prohibited to protect brand identity.
*   **Transactional / Money URLs**: URLs that directly drive commercial conversions must remain locked. Structural changes to these pages require multi-department review.

---

## 6. Cost/Value Thinking

Refreshes should be prioritized based on projected return on investment (ROI):

$$\text{Refit ROI} = \frac{\text{Historical Peak Traffic Value} - \text{Current Traffic Value}}{\text{Copywriter Hours} \times \text{Hourly Rate}}$$

*   **High Value / Low Effort**: Metadata rewrites for Striking Distance pages (`POS-03`, `CTR-04`) take less than 15 minutes but can restore substantial traffic. These must always be executed first.
*   **High Value / High Effort**: Comprehensive content refreshes for high-exposure old pages (`DEC-01`, `AGE-02`) require 2-4 hours of writing time. These should be batched and prioritized for high-impact commercial assets.

---

## 7. Monitoring & Retraining Triggers

To prevent model performance decay (model drift), the scoring queue should be updated and the underlying classifiers retrained upon encountering the following triggers:

*   **Trigger A: Core Algorithm Update**: Major Google search engine core updates alter baseline click distributions, requiring model retraining using post-update datasets.
*   **Trigger B: 90-Day Cadence**: Retrain the classifiers quarterly to capture seasonal query shifts and newly indexed page parameters.
*   **Trigger C: Performance Drift**: If Precision@50 measured on live content updates drops below 70% for two consecutive feedback loops.
